title: System Configuration
description: System-scope configuration (config/system.json). The system-wide layer of the layered configuration model (RD-5): structured JSON validated by this published schema. All Tier-3 instance constants (reviewer ids, channel-role bindings) land here, never in code. Spec ref: release-spec.md §11.2.

schema_version.description: Version of this config shape, for migration handling (§11.1).

mode.description: Publish posture ladder. Fresh installs default to SAFE (fail-closed). LIVE requires an operational project plus an explicit config change.

reviewers.description: The approval allowlist (DD-17). MUST be non-empty with at least one reviewer holding approve rights. Every approval/edit decision is attributed to an entry here; edits require the same rights as approvals.

budget.description: Spend governance (DD-18). REQUIRED: the engine refuses LIVE mode without it. Caps bind engine-metered spend and run dispatch only; chain-seat LLM spend is capped at the host runtime (see docs/cost.md). A cap breach forces the project to paused (§15.4).

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for all cap values.

monthly_cap.description: Hard monthly ceiling on engine-metered spend. NOT a whole-system ceiling unless the host runtime reports chain spend.

daily_cap.description: Hard daily ceiling on engine-metered spend.

per_item_generation_limit.description: Maximum engine-metered spend permitted producing a single content item.

indexing_requires_estimate.description: When true (required), engine index-library and engine calibrate MUST present a pre-run cost estimate and require confirmation before spending.

publish.description: Publish posture (the canonical home of the diagnostic publish-flag overrides). draft_only true means approved posts land as drafts the operator publishes manually (the second gate).

draft_only.description: When true (default), publisher handoff creates a draft; the operator publishes it manually.

auto_publish_allowed.description: Global gate for per-brand auto-publish. Even when true, each brand×platform must independently earn the trust state in brand.json (§15.5).

approval_surface.description: The approval surface adapter and its channel-role bindings. Channel ids are Tier-3 instance constants supplied by the operator; templates ship placeholders only.

adapter.description: Approval-surface adapter id. v1 reference implementation is discord.

channels.description: Channel-role bindings by id. Required roles: content-review, content-published, content-ops, media-bank. trend-readout is optional.

scheduler.description: Trigger schedule. Safety posture never lives in scheduler wrappers; it lives here in declared config.

ttl_sweep_interval_minutes.description: Approval-card TTL sweep interval in minutes.

tick_enabled.description: Enable the optional fine-grained calendar-tick trigger. Ships off by default; the daily kickoff is the canonical trigger.

trends.description: The TREND SOURCE pathway (release-spec §8.8; DD-16). CONFIG-GATED and OFF BY DEFAULT: the config-gated trend pass polls the configured adapter on a 2/4/8/12h cadence, posts an angles-only readout to the trend-readout channel, and dispatches one seed per fresh report into a RESERVED trend calendar slot (slot_type=trend) — never out-of-calendar (DD-16). It NEVER auto-publishes; the seed runs the full chain to the human approval card (§2.4). Scraping is BYO (RD-9); no creds are bundled.

enabled.description: The OFF-by-default gate (the LAW). Strictly true to enable; any other value keeps the pathway disabled (fail-closed).

adapter.description: Registered trend-source adapter name to poll (shipped: 'reference' for a BYO provider, 'fixture' for zero-key tests; or an operator-registered adapter). Required when enabled to poll an adapter; the manual-submission path (a trend-report file dropped into $CONTENT_HOME/trends/) needs no adapter.

cadence.description: How often the scheduler recipe invokes the trend pass. Carried into the adapter poll and used as the report freshness-window basis (DD-15). Default 12h (the most conservative).

themes.description: Optional theme/topic hints passed to the adapter to focus the poll.

provider.description: Adapter-specific provider configuration (a §12.5-style block, opaque to the engine; e.g. { endpoint, platform, auth_scheme }). The reference adapter resolves its credential BY NAME via secrets.js (RD-9 BYO); no credential VALUE ever lives here.

private_terms.description: Extra redaction deny-list terms applied to report bodies at write (the privacy pre-pass, §13.3). Public anti-targets, not secrets.

brand_dna.description: The DATA-INGESTION & BRAND-IDENTITY pathway (release-spec §1.1 Data Ingestion & Brand Identity; §1.2 Context & Competitor Analysis; roadmap #2). CONFIG-GATED and OFF BY DEFAULT: the C2 setup checkpoint runs a ONE-COMMAND flow that ingests the operator account corpus + the competitor corpus, runs a DETERMINISTIC corpus analyzer (auditable stats — NO LLM), categorizes archetypes, then OPTIONALLY synthesizes a brand-dna.md via a HOST seat the runtime wires. ENGINE NEVER CALLS CHAIN/ANALYSIS LLMs DIRECTLY (RD-2): DNA synthesis is an injectable host seat (the §12.5 vision-seam pattern); it degrades gracefully to the manual authoring template (templates/brand/brand-dna-authoring.md) when no seat is wired (DD-21 cold-start — onboarding is NEVER blocked). SCRAPING POSTURE (RD-9): manual submission + official-account EXPORTS are first-class; scraping is BYO adapter — NO creds bundled, the operator is the data controller, ToS responsibility is the operator's. Ingested corpus is Zone U (RD-8, untrusted third-party incl. competitors), trust-class-tagged at write to $CONTENT_HOME/corpora/<brand>/ (corpus-item.schema.json) and governed by the system `retention` block (engine purge-corpora). Competitor content is analyzed for PATTERNS only — NEVER republished verbatim; a check enforces that the derived DNA/archetypes carry no copied competitor copy.

enabled.description: The OFF-by-default gate (the LAW). Strictly true to enable the one-command ingestion+analysis flow in the C2 checkpoint; any other value keeps the pathway disabled (fail-closed). Even when false, the manual authoring path (templates/brand/brand-dna-authoring.md) and manual corpus submission still work — onboarding is never blocked (DD-21).

scraper.description: OPTIONAL BYO scraper adapter for corpus ingestion (RD-9). Absent means manual submission + official-account EXPORTS only (the first-class path). When present, the adapter is resolved BY NAME from the registry; its credential (if any) is resolved BY NAME via secrets.js — NO credential VALUE ever lives here, and the repo bundles NO creds. Scraping is a METERED action: the engine presents a pre-run cost estimate + confirm before any scrape (DD-18). The operator is the data controller and owns ToS responsibility (RD-9, disclosed in docs/data-policy.md).

adapter.description: Registered scraper-adapter name (shipped: 'reference' for a BYO provider, 'fixture' for zero-key tests; or an operator-registered adapter). The manual-submission/export path needs no adapter.

provider.description: Adapter-specific provider configuration (a §12.5-style block, opaque to the engine; e.g. { endpoint, platform, auth_scheme }). The reference adapter resolves its credential BY NAME via secrets.js (RD-9 BYO); no credential VALUE ever lives here.

max_items_per_handle.description: Cap on items pulled per handle in one scrape (bounds the metered spend the cost estimate quotes). Default 200.

synthesis.description: OPTIONAL host DNA-SYNTHESIS seat wiring. The seat turns the deterministic analysis into brand-DNA voice prose; it is a HOST-runtime seat (RD-2), INJECTABLE for tests (the §12.5 vision-seam pattern). DNA synthesis is a METERED action: the engine presents a pre-run cost estimate + confirm before invoking the seat (DD-18). Absent / no seat wired => the flow degrades gracefully: it emits the deterministic analysis + the authoring template for the agent to finish (DD-21).

seat.description: Registered DNA-synthesis seat name the runtime wires (shipped: 'fixture' for zero-key tests; or a host-registered seat). When absent, the flow degrades to the manual authoring template.

provider.description: Seat-specific provider configuration (a §12.5-style block, opaque to the engine). Credentials resolve BY NAME via secrets.js (RD-2/RD-9); no credential VALUE here.

synthesis_usd.description: OPTIONAL cost band (USD) the DD-18 pre-run estimate quotes for the metered DNA-synthesis seat. Falls back to config.cost.per_synthesis_usd, then an engine default. low/high bound the quoted estimate; the actual spend a real seat reports is capped by the §11.2 budget.

verbatim_shingle_words.description: OPTIONAL word n-gram (shingle) width the no-verbatim-republish check uses to detect copied competitor copy in the generated DNA/archetypes (the RD-9 enforcement). Minimum 3; engine default applies when absent.

private_terms.description: Extra redaction deny-list terms applied to the generated brand-dna.md / archetype catalog at write (the privacy pre-pass, §13.3). Public anti-targets (partner names / codenames), not secrets.

work_recap.description: The WORK-RECAP / build-in-public SOURCE pathway (release-spec §2.1 seeding; §3.3 operator/founder/team accounts with flexible voice). CONFIG-GATED and OFF BY DEFAULT: the daily kickoff fills one build-in-public option per day per OPERATOR account from a CONFIGURED, SENSITIVE memory path. PRIVACY IS LOAD-BEARING — the source runs a redaction/privacy pre-pass (shared/redact + this private-term deny list) and the draft passes the gate's privacy/leak check BEFORE the human approval card (§2.4). The repo ships the MECHANISM pointed at a configured path; it NEVER bundles or commits real memory. Recaps fill RESERVED work_recap calendar slots, never out-of-calendar.

enabled.description: The OFF-by-default gate (the LAW). Strictly true to enable; any other value keeps the pathway disabled (fail-closed).

memory_path.description: Absolute or process-relative path to the SENSITIVE project memory the source scans (read-only; never copied into the repo). Block-level default; per-account entries may override it. Required (block- or account-level) when enabled.

files.description: Files-glob list relative to memory_path that the source scans. Supports an exact file and a single 'dir/*.ext' wildcard. Default ['MEMORY.md', 'memory/*.md'].

lookback_days.description: How many days back the scan considers recent work. Default 3.

max_items.description: Cap on extracted work items per seed (keeps a recap focused). Default 40.

private_terms.description: The config-extendable private-term deny list (partner names, codenames, unreleased feature names). Threaded into the privacy pre-pass AND onto the seed so the gate's privacy/leak check enforces it. Public anti-targets to NOT print — not secrets themselves (secret VALUES are stripped by redaction).

extra_secret_keys.description: Extra sensitive key NAMES the privacy/leak gate treats as secret-bearing (extends the built-in redact.js secret shapes for instance-specific key names). Names only, never secret values.

brand.description: Default operator/founder/team account this recap targets (registered as a brand with account_class=operator, §3.3/§6.4). Used when accounts[] is absent (single-account mode).

account.description: Default operator/founder/team account handle/ref (Tier-3) the build-in-public post targets. Used when accounts[] is absent.

accounts.description: Per-account work-recap settings (§3.3: each operator/founder/team account gets its own daily build-in-public option). Each entry inherits the block-level memory_path/files/lookback_days/private_terms defaults unless it overrides them. When absent, the block's own brand/account is the single account.

cooldown.description: Media reuse cooldown defaults (per-brand overridable in brand.json).

hard_days.description: Hard floor: an asset or its derivatives MUST NOT be reused more often than this.

gate.description: Deterministic-gate tunables. The engine ships generic day-one defaults in code (engine/gate/pre-gate-lint.js, §10.3); the operator/maintainer's calibrated values live here and are NOT shipped (DD-9).

variant_distinctness.description: Variant-duplication thresholds (rule.core.variant). Two variants fire LINT.VARIANT_DUP when their n-gram body similarity reaches jaccard_threshold or they share the opener window. Shipped defaults are generic starting points, not calibrated values.

shingle_n.description: Word n-gram (shingle) size for body-overlap similarity.

jaccard_threshold.description: Body-overlap similarity at/above which two variants are too similar. Generic default; tune per brand voice.

opener_chars.description: Leading-character window compared for identical openers.

trend.description: Trend-card TTL. The token 'freshness-window-bound' means TTL is inherited from the originating trend report's freshness window.

evergreen_escalate_after.description: Age after which an evergreen card escalates rather than silently expiring.

retention.description: Corpus retention windows + purge schedule (RD-9). The operator is the data controller; engine purge-corpora honors retention_class.

raw_corpus_days.description: Default retention (days) for retention_class:standard / untagged raw third-party corpora before purge eligibility.

transient_days.description: Retention (days) for retention_class:transient corpus items (the shortest window). engine purge-corpora purges transient items older than this.

purge_schedule.description: Cadence at which engine purge-corpora is expected to run (e.g. daily, weekly).

observability.description: Health-surface configuration: digest timing and stall thresholds (§13).

stall_thresholds.description: Thresholds that drive heartbeat/stall alerts. Silent death MUST NOT be the failure presentation.

no_content_produced_hours.description: Alert if no content has been produced within this many hours.

queue_age_alert_hours.description: Alert if cards remain pending past this age.

undelivered_card_backlog.description: When undelivered-card backlog exceeds this count, new generation pauses (degraded-mode guard).

paused.description: Mirror of the PAUSED sentinel. When true, all autonomous loops are paused (kill switch, §15.4).

id.description: Reviewer identity on the approval surface (Tier-3). Resolvable by the surface adapter.

rights.description: Granted rights. At least one reviewer in the allowlist MUST have 'approve'.

channel_id.description: Approval-surface channel identifier (Tier-3 instance constant). Operator-supplied; templates use placeholders such as <CHANNEL_ID>.

duration.description: Duration token, an integer followed by a unit (h hours, d days, m minutes).

trend_cadence.description: Trend-poll cadence token (§8.8). One of the supported polling intervals; off-list values are rejected fail-closed by the engine.

private_terms.description: A private-term deny list: PUBLIC anti-targets (terms NOT to print, e.g. partner names / codenames / unreleased feature names). NEVER secret VALUES — those are stripped by redaction. Either a flat string array, or a { terms, secret_literals } object (both forbidden-to-print).

work_recap_account.description: One operator/founder/team account's work-recap settings (§3.3). brand/account scope it; memory_path/files/lookback_days/private_terms override the block-level defaults when present.

brand.description: The operator-class brand id this account's recap targets (account_class=operator).

account.description: The account handle/ref (Tier-3) the build-in-public post targets.

memory_path.description: Per-account memory path override (else the block-level work_recap.memory_path).

files.description: Per-account files-glob override (else the block-level default).

lookback_days.description: Per-account lookback override (else the block-level default).

private_terms.description: Per-account extra deny-list terms, UNIONED with the block-level private_terms.
